summary.intent: Implement Gemini thought_signature round-tripping (#375) by adding an opaque metadata field to ToolCall, and strengthen schema validation (#793) to fix Google AI Studio required-array failures when routing via OpenRouter.
summary.outcome: All core struct changes, provider extraction logic, persistence layer updates, and ~32 files with ToolCall construction sites were updated. The build compiled successfully after iterative compiler-driven fixes across both source and test code.

repo: ToolCall is constructed in ~80+ places across the codebase; adding a new required field causes cascading compile errors that must be fixed compiler-driven across agents, providers, chat, and test code.
StreamEvent::ToolCallStart is matched in ~29 files; new fields require destructuring or `..` ignoring at every match site.

The openai_compat provider handles both non-streaming (parse_tool_calls) and streaming (process_openai_sse_line) paths separately, plus a third Responses API path — all three need independent metadata extraction.
serde(default, skip_serializing_if) on PersistedToolCall fields provides backward compatibility with existing stored sessions without migration.
build_persisted_tool_call and build_tool_call_assistant_message in crates/chat/src/service/types.rs are the sole persistence entry points for tool calls.
The codebase uses a TOOL_CALL_METADATA_KEYS allowlist pattern to avoid bloating session storage with arbitrary provider fields.
Provider-specific metadata (thought_signature) is extracted at parse time and re-injected into to_openai_value() output, completing the round-trip without touching intermediate business logic.

code.finding: ToolCall struct — added Optional metadata Map field for provider-specific round-tripping
StreamEvent::ToolCallStart — added metadata field to propagate from SSE chunk to ToolCall construction
to_openai_value() — merges metadata fields back into tool_calls JSON array for API requests
values_to_chat_messages() — calls extract_tool_call_metadata() to recover metadata from persisted JSON
parse_tool_calls() — extracts thought_signature from non-streaming Gemini responses
process_openai_sse_line() — extracts metadata from first SSE chunk of each tool call where id and function.name appear
PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform strengthened with has_usable_type() check requiring type/enum/anyOf/oneOf/allOf/$ref
PersistedToolCall — added metadata field with serde(default, skip_serializing_if) for session backward compatibility
build_persisted_tool_call() and build_tool_call_assistant_message() updated to accept and thread metadata parameter

workflow: Compiler-driven development is effective for large struct field additions: add field, build, fix all errors surfaced, repeat until clean.
Spawning parallel sub-agents to fix test files in bulk (22+ errors across 10+ files) is an efficient strategy when changes are mechanical (add metadata: None).
Running cargo build --tests separately from cargo build catches test-only compilation errors that don't appear in a regular build.
When a lint error says 'unnecessary qualification', it means the type is already in scope via a use statement and the full path should be shortened.
The iterative build cycle was: core types → providers → chat layer → test code → integration tests, each round surfacing the next layer of errors.

friction: cargo build output required multiple reads to extract error locations — no single command gave all errors cleanly in one pass.
The serde_json::Map qualification triggered a lint error in types.rs because serde_json::Value was already imported as Value but Map was not.
22 test-code compile errors appeared only after the main build passed, requiring a second build pass with --tests flag.
Multiple rounds of parallel agent dispatches were needed to fix test files because errors appeared in waves across different test modules.
The streaming SSE path in openai_compat required understanding that metadata only appears in the first chunk (where id and function.name are present), not in subsequent delta chunks.
open_items: EnsurePropertyTypeTransform (infer 'type': 'string' for required properties with description but no type) was specified in the plan but not confirmed as implemented in the transcript.
New e2e Playwright tests (tool-call-metadata.spec.js and schema-validation.spec.js) were listed in the plan but not shown as created in the transcript.
gemini_integration.rs multi_turn_tool_use test was supposed to be updated to remove the thought_signature-expected-failure path — not confirmed in transcript.
Unit tests for ToolCall metadata round-trip, PersistedToolCall backward compat, streaming metadata extraction, and schema normalization were specified but not shown as written.
StreamingToolState was supposed to gain a tool_call_metadata HashMap to carry metadata through streaming — unclear if fully implemented or just threaded via ToolCallStart.
The allowlist TOOL_CALL_METADATA_KEYS is hardcoded to ['thought_signature']; future provider-specific fields will need manual additions to this list.
